name: Apple MLX Framework
what_it_does: Apple MLX is an open-source machine learning framework designed for Apple Silicon (M1/M2/M3/M4 chips). Enables fast local inference of LLMs, image models, and other ML models on Mac hardware using the unified memory architecture. Used for running models like Llama, Mistral, and Phi locally on Apple hardware without GPU clouds.
use_cases: Running LLMs locally on Mac for privacy-sensitive agent workloads
Zero-cost inference for agents on Apple Silicon (no API costs)
Fine-tuning models on Mac for custom agent use cases
Development and prototyping without API dependencies or internet
Edge deployment of AI agents on Apple devices (Mac Mini as inference server)
not_for: Windows or Linux inference (Apple Silicon only)
Large models >70B that exceed Mac unified memory
best_when: You're developing on Mac, want local inference without GPU cloud costs, and need a developer-friendly framework for Apple Silicon.
avoid_when: You need cloud inference, Windows/Linux support, or production-scale deployment beyond a single Mac.

auth.notes: Local library — no authentication needed. Runs entirely on device.

pricing.notes: Free and open source. Only cost is the Mac hardware and electricity.

performance.rate_limits: Limited by Apple Silicon hardware performance

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: Python library with standard exceptions. Memory overflow errors clearly indicate model is too large for hardware. API is Pythonic and errors follow Python conventions.
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: Local inference is fully controllable — same seed produces same output.
known_agent_gotchas: No REST API — agents must use MLX as a Python library, not an HTTP service
Memory limits: 7B model needs ~4GB, 13B needs ~8GB, 70B needs ~40GB unified memory
Model loading time: 30-60 seconds for initial load — design for warm models
Not all model architectures are supported — check compatibility before selecting model
Requires Apple Silicon — M1 Pro minimum for useful inference speeds
No built-in model serving — need to add FastAPI/Flask wrapper for HTTP access
Metal compute shaders may need macOS version updates
agent_readiness.security_notes: Maximum privacy — all computation local. No network calls. Data never leaves device. Best possible security posture.
